title: Document Monthly Runtime terminology and test-user runbook
description: Document the product and execution terminology before external test users see Monthly Runtime. Public docs must separate TechStack Standard/Premium from Simulate managed/BYOK and permanent/one-time lanes.
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain TechStack monthly-runtime-standard and monthly-runtime-premium as subscription runtimes without token model; Simulate managed/BYOK and permanent/one-time are documented as separate axes; the Base Kit test-user runbook covers lease, enrollment, bootstrap, status, SSH, Stop/Start, verification, and cleanup; terminology matches internal roadmap and Beads.

title: Clean up Mintlify StackKits guide pages
description: Clean up the newly added StackKits Node Hub and service-guide MDX pages so the Mintlify navigation, guide structure, public tone, and validation gates stay consistent.

title: Adopt Kombify local testing standard
description:
Keep this repo aligned with the shared Kombify local testing standard.

Scope:
- Keep docs/LOCAL_TESTING.md as the canonical local testing and local deployment entrypoint when this repo has local runtime or deployment instructions.
- Keep README.md and docs entrypoints linked to docs/LOCAL_TESTING.md instead of duplicating divergent commands.
- Use mise as the repo-local command contract for setup, doctor, build, test, check, health, preview:local, preflight:quick, preflight:release, preflight:deploy, aspire:start, and aspire:full.
- Use Docker Desktop for preflight:release component previews and Aspire for multi-repo local, SaaS-like, self-hosted, hybrid, embedded, and standalone scenarios through the shared .github gate when the repo participates in the gate manifest.
- Use local preflight receipts with --report before remote CI or deploy work.
- For Beads-active repos outside .github/local-dev/kombify-gates.json, either add the repo to the shared gate manifest or document why it is intentionally out of local gate scope.
acceptance_criteria:
- mise run preflight:quick remains the default pre-commit check when the repo has executable code.
- mise run preflight:release remains the default pre-PR or pre-push check when the repo has a local preview/runtime.
- mise run preflight:deploy remains the required pre-CI or deploy check when the repo participates in remote CI/deploy.
- For manifest repos, .github audit passes and shared manifest dry-runs for quick, standard, full, up, smoke, and down keep resolving.
- For non-manifest Beads repos, the repo is either added to the shared local gate manifest or explicitly documented as out of local gate scope.
- README.md and docs/* do not reintroduce obsolete direct local deploy or test instructions that bypass docs/LOCAL_TESTING.md.
